text:
Daniel Wiffen says he was ‘incredibly disappointed’ to miss out on the chance to be Ireland’s flagbearer forthe closing ceremony of the Paris Olympicsafter being ‘rushed to hospital’ with an illness.

The 23-year-old swimmer clinched gold in the men’s 800m freestyle competition, setting a new Olympic record in the process, to add to the two World Championship titles he won in Doha earlier this year.

He also claimed the bronze medal in the men’s 1,500m freestyle and became Ireland’s first ever athlete to compete in the 10km open-water marathon, finishing in 18th place.

However, after the gruelling swim in the River Seine,Wiffen described the experience as ‘probably one of the worst things I’ve done’due to being repeatedly ‘punched and kicked in the face’.

The race went ahead despite major concern surrounding the quality of the water in the river, withthe men’s triathlon having to be postponed as a result of high levels of E.coli– which can cause pneumonia, sepsis or even meningitis.

Several triathletes, including Vasco Vilaca, Hayden Wilde and Ainsley Thorpe, have since come down with illnesses and Wiffen was forced to miss last night’s closing ceremony after being hospitalised with a ‘bug’.

It is not known whether Wiffen’s sickness is related to his participation in the open-water marathon.

Thanks everyone who reached out, Iâm incredibly disappointed to miss out on the opportunity to be flag bearer last night.Yesterday I rushed to hospital as I was very unwell with a bug that I am being treated for, and am feeling better now. I hope everyone enjoys the eveningâ¦

‘Thanks everyone who reached out, I’m incredibly disappointed to miss out on the opportunity to be flagbearer last night,’ Wiffen wrote on X, formerly known as Twitter.

‘Yesterday I rushed to hospital as I was very unwell with a bug that I am being treated for, and am feeling better now.

‘I hope everyone enjoys the evening and I hope to be well enough to seeing everyone when we get home.’

Double gold-medalist Fintan McCarthy replaced Wiffen as Ireland’s flagbearer and led out his nation alongside bronze-winning swimmer Mona McSharry at the Stade de France.

In a separate post, Wiffen said he had realised a ‘childhood dream’ by representing Ireland at an Olympic Games.

‘They say every child has a dream, to pursue the dream is in every child hand’s to make it a reality. I had the dream of becoming an Olympic Champion.

‘I put the work in every day and I’m so happy to say that I have accomplished my childhood dream.

‘However, behind every child you have a support team of friends, family, coaches, teammates who encourage and help make your dream a reality.

‘So I would firstly like to thank my parents who sacrificed their own lives to make my dream a reality.

‘My best friends and teammates who supported and pushed me to achieve my dream.

‘My coaches, especially Andi Manley you created the journey and allowed me to push myself until I had achieved my dream.

Wiffen added: ‘Now Paris 2024 is over and I head back to Ireland I can finally reflect and thank everyone for the support across the past weeks and I’m happy to say I’m bringing a Gold and Bronze home

‘Slainte mo chairde [good health friends].’

title: Ireland's Olympic hero Daniel Wiffen 'rushed to hospital' before closing ceremony | Metro News
description: Daniel Wiffen says he was 'disappointed' to miss out on the chance to be Ireland's Olympic flagbearer after being 'rushed to hospital'.
